name: Satwik & Chirag
bio: Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty — India's first-ever men's doubles pair to break into the BWF top 5. Singapore Open 2025 champions, Asian Games gold medallists, and the form pair on tour heading into Indonesia.

name: Lakshya Sen
bio: Lakshya Sen — All England finalist, Paris Olympics fourth-place. Quick on his feet, aggressive on the net. Carrying India's hopes in men's singles after the Lakshya–Prannoy generation handover.

name: PV Sindhu
bio: Pusarla Venkata Sindhu — two-time Olympic medallist, World Championship gold (2019). After an injury-disrupted 2025, she's quietly rebuilding her engine for the 2028 Olympic cycle.

name: Treesa & Gayatri
bio: Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand — India's most promising women's doubles pair in a decade. Career-high ranking and a string of deep runs at Super 500+ events.

name: HS Prannoy
bio: Haseena Sunil Prannoy Kumar — Indian Open 2023 champion, World Championship bronze (2023). Known for grinding out three-game thrillers; the elder statesman in India's singles room.

name: Malvika Bansod
bio: Malvika Bansod — left-handed, deceptive, climbing the rankings ladder steadily. Won her maiden senior international title in 2022 and has been chipping away at the world top 30 since.
